pottery.name: Pottery
pottery.description: Enables food storage and early civilization development

bronze_working.name: Bronze Working
bronze_working.description: Metal working enables stronger weapons and tools

horseback_riding.name: Horseback Riding
horseback_riding.description: Domestication of horses for transportation and warfare

the_wheel.name: The Wheel
the_wheel.description: Revolutionary invention enabling transportation and machinery

chivalry.name: Chivalry
chivalry.description: Code of honor for mounted warriors

invention.name: Invention
invention.description: Systematic approach to creating new technologies

philosophy.name: Philosophy
philosophy.description: Systematic thinking about existence and knowledge

physics.name: Physics
physics.description: Understanding of natural forces and matter

chemistry.name: Chemistry
chemistry.description: Study of matter and chemical reactions

explosives.name: Explosives
explosives.description: Powerful explosive compounds for mining and warfare

refining.name: Refining
refining.description: Processing of raw materials into useful products

religion.name: Religion
religion.description: Organized spiritual beliefs and practices that unite communities

atomic_theory.name: Atomic Theory
atomic_theory.description: Understanding of atomic structure and forces

nuclear_fission.name: Nuclear Fission
nuclear_fission.description: Splitting atoms for energy and weapons

recycling.name: Recycling
recycling.description: Reuse of materials to reduce waste

fusion_power.name: Fusion Power
fusion_power.description: Unlimited clean energy from nuclear fusion

barracks.name: Barracks
barracks.description: Military training facility. All units produced in this city start as veterans.

granary.name: Granary
granary.description: Food storage facility. Reduces food needed for population growth by 50%.

temple.name: Temple
temple.description: Religious building that makes citizens happier and prevents volcanic disasters.

palace.name: Palace
palace.description: Seat of government. Reduces corruption throughout your empire.

walls.name: City Walls
walls.description: Fortifications that triple the defense of units in the city and prevent floods.

library.name: Library
library.description: Center of learning that increases science production by 50%.

marketplace.name: Marketplace
marketplace.description: Trading center that increases tax revenue and luxuries by 50%.

courthouse.name: Courthouse
courthouse.description: Legal center that reduces corruption in the city by 50%.

aqueduct.name: Aqueduct
aqueduct.description: Water system that allows cities to grow beyond size 10 and prevents fire and plague.

colosseum.name: Colosseum
colosseum.description: Entertainment venue that makes 3 unhappy citizens content.

bank.name: Bank
bank.description: Financial institution that increases tax revenue and luxuries by 50%. Requires Marketplace.

cathedral.name: Cathedral
cathedral.description: Great religious building that makes 4 unhappy citizens content.

university.name: University
university.description: Higher learning institution that increases science by 50%. Requires Library.

sewer_system.name: Sewer System
sewer_system.description: Sanitation system that allows cities to grow beyond size 12. Requires Aqueduct.

factory.name: Factory
factory.description: Industrial facility that increases production by 50%.

power_plant.name: Power Plant
power_plant.description: Coal-fired power plant that doubles factory/manufacturing plant bonuses.

hydro_plant.name: Hydro Plant
hydro_plant.description: Clean hydroelectric power that doubles factory bonuses and reduces pollution. Requires water access.

nuclear_plant.name: Nuclear Plant
nuclear_plant.description: Nuclear power plant that doubles factory bonuses. Risk of meltdown if city in disorder.

mass_transit.name: Mass Transit
mass_transit.description: Public transportation that eliminates pollution from population.

recycling_center.name: Recycling Center
recycling_center.description: Waste management facility that reduces pollution by two-thirds.

mfg_plant.name: Manufacturing Plant
mfg_plant.description: Advanced factory that increases production by 100%. Makes regular factories obsolete.

colossus.name: Colossus
colossus.description: A massive bronze statue that brings extra trade to squares with existing trade.
effects: One extra trade arrow on squares where there is already at least one trade arrow

great_library.name: Great Library
great_library.description: The greatest repository of knowledge in the ancient world.
effects: Receive any technology that any two other civilizations have

great_wall.name: Great Wall
great_wall.description: A massive fortification that ensures peaceful relations with other civilizations.
effects: Other leaders always offer a peace treaty

hanging_gardens.name: Hanging Gardens
hanging_gardens.description: One of the seven wonders of the ancient world. Makes one content person happy in all cities.
effects: One content person becomes happy in all cities

lighthouse.name: Lighthouse
lighthouse.description: A beacon that guides ships safely to harbor, improving naval movement.
effects: +1 movement for ships

oracle.name: Oracle
oracle.description: Ancient shrine that doubles the effect of temples in all cities.
effects: Doubles the effect of temples in all cities

pyramids.name: Pyramids
pyramids.description: Magnificent tombs that allow switching to any government with only one turn of Anarchy.
effects: Switch to any government with only one turn of Anarchy

copernicus_observatory.name: Copernicus' Observatory
copernicus_observatory.description: A center of astronomical learning that doubles science production in its city.
effects: Increases science production by 100% in the city

darwins_voyage.name: Darwin's Voyage
darwins_voyage.description: Revolutionary scientific discoveries that provide immediate technological advances.
effects: Two Civilization Advances are discovered immediately

isaac_newtons_college.name: Isaac Newton's College
isaac_newtons_college.description: A center of learning that increases the effectiveness of Libraries and Universities.
effects: Increases Library and University effects from 50% each to 83% each in all cities

js_bachs_cathedral.name: J.S. Bach's Cathedral
js_bachs_cathedral.description: Beautiful music that makes unhappy people content on the same continent.
effects: 2 unhappy people become content in all cities on the same continent

magellans_expedition.name: Magellan's Expedition
magellans_expedition.description: The first circumnavigation of the globe improves naval movement.
effects: +1 movement for ships

michelangelos_chapel.name: Michelangelo's Chapel
michelangelos_chapel.description: Magnificent artwork that increases Cathedral effectiveness by 50%.
effects: Cathedral effect increases by 50% in all cities

shakespeares_theatre.name: Shakespeare's Theatre
shakespeares_theatre.description: The greatest theatrical works make all unhappy citizens content in its city.
effects: All unhappy citizens become content in the city

apollo_program.name: Apollo Program
apollo_program.description: Enables spaceship construction and reveals all cities in the world.
effects: Spaceship parts may be built
Reveals every city in the world

cure_for_cancer.name: Cure for Cancer
cure_for_cancer.description: A medical breakthrough that makes one content person happy in all cities.
effects: One content person becomes happy in all cities

hoover_dam.name: Hoover Dam
hoover_dam.description: A massive hydroelectric project that provides hydro power to all cities on the same continent.
effects: Supplies hydro power (acts as hydroplant) in all cities on the same continent

manhattan_project.name: Manhattan Project
manhattan_project.description: The development of atomic weapons enables nuclear unit construction for all civilizations.
effects: Nuclear units may be built by all civilizations with Rocketry

seti_program.name: SETI Program
seti_program.description: The search for extraterrestrial intelligence increases science production.
effects: Increases science production by 50% in all cities

united_nations.name: United Nations
united_nations.description: An international organization that ensures other leaders always offer peace.
effects: Other leaders always offer a peace treaty

romans.name: Roman Empire
romans.description: A powerful empire that dominated the Mediterranean world through military might and administrative excellence.

american.name: Americans
american.description: A young nation founded on principles of democracy and freedom, destined for expansion across a vast continent.

aztecs.name: Aztec Empire
aztecs.description: A mighty Mesoamerican empire built on tribute, trade, and religious devotion centered in the Valley of Mexico.

babylonian.name: Babylonians
babylonian.description: Ancient masters of law and astronomy from Mesopotamia, the cradle of civilization between the rivers.

chinese.name: Chinese
chinese.description: An ancient civilization known for technological innovation, philosophy, and the Mandate of Heaven.

egyptian.name: Egyptians
egyptian.description: Masters of the Nile, builders of pyramids and monuments that have endured for millennia.

english.name: English
english.description: A maritime power destined to rule the waves and establish a global empire upon which the sun never sets.

french.name: French
french.description: A nation of culture, cuisine, and conquest that has shaped European politics and philosophy for centuries.

german.name: Germans
german.description: A confederation of industrious peoples known for engineering excellence, philosophy, and military precision.

greeks.name: Greeks
greeks.description: The birthplace of democracy, philosophy, and classical learning that laid the foundation of Western civilization.

indian.name: Indians
indian.description: A diverse subcontinent rich in spirituality, mathematics, and trade that bridges East and West.

mongol.name: Mongols
mongol.description: Fierce nomadic warriors who built the largest contiguous land empire in history through superior horsemanship and tactics.

russian.name: Russians
russian.description: A vast empire spanning continents, enduring through harsh winters and emerging as a major world power.

zulu.name: Zulus
zulu.description: A proud warrior nation of southern Africa known for military innovation and fierce resistance to colonization.

barbarians.name: Barbarians
barbarians.description: Roving raiders that threaten all civilizations.

1.title: Reign of Augustus
1.description: Augustus Caesar rules the Roman Empire at its height, establishing the Pax Romana and expanding trade across the known world.

100.title: Roman Peace
100.description: The Pax Romana flourishes under the rule of the Roman Empire at its height.

200.title: Three Kingdoms China
200.description: China fractures into three kingdoms following the collapse of the Han Dynasty.

300.title: Rise of Christianity
300.description: Christianity spreads throughout the Roman Empire, eventually becoming the dominant religion.

400.title: Fall of Rome
400.description: The Roman Empire in the West collapses, marking the beginning of the Middle Ages in Europe.

500.title: Dark Ages
500.description: Europe enters the Early Middle Ages with feudalism emerging as the dominant system.

600.title: Islamic Golden Age
600.description: Islam spreads rapidly across the Middle East and North Africa under the Umayyad Caliphate.

700.title: Tang Dynasty
700.description: The Tang Dynasty rules China during a period of cultural and economic prosperity.

800.title: Charlemagne
800.description: Charlemagne is crowned Emperor of the Romans, establishing the Carolingian Empire.

900.title: Medieval Europe
900.description: The feudal system dominates medieval Europe with kings, nobles, and peasants in strict hierarchy.

1000.title: Millennium Change
1000.description: Medieval Europe experiences significant cultural and technological developments.

1100.title: The Crusades
1100.description: European Christians launch military campaigns to reclaim the Holy Land from Muslim rule.

1200.title: Magna Carta Era
1200.description: King John of England signs the Magna Carta, limiting royal power and establishing rights.

1300.title: Renaissance Begins
1300.description: The Renaissance begins in Italy, marking the rebirth of classical learning and culture.

1400.title: Age of Exploration
1400.description: European explorers like Columbus and da Gama discover new routes and continents.

1500.title: Gutenberg's Press
1500.description: The printing press revolutionizes communication, spreading knowledge throughout Europe.

1600.title: Scientific Revolution
1600.description: Scientists like Galileo and Kepler revolutionize our understanding of the universe.

1700.title: Enlightenment
1700.description: The Age of Enlightenment emphasizes reason, science, and individual rights.

1750.title: Industrial Revolution
1750.description: The Industrial Revolution begins in Britain, transforming manufacturing and society.

1800.title: Napoleonic Era
1800.description: Napoleon Bonaparte reshapes Europe through military conquest and legal reforms.

1850.title: Victorian Age
1850.description: The Victorian Era in Britain is marked by industrial progress and imperial expansion.

1900.title: Belle Époque
1900.description: The early 20th century marks an era of peace and cultural flourishing in Europe.

1920.title: Roaring Twenties
1920.description: The 1920s bring jazz, flappers, and economic prosperity to America and Europe.

1945.title: World War II Ends
1945.description: World War II concludes, reshaping global politics and leading to the nuclear age.

1969.title: Moon Landing
1969.description: Apollo 11 lands on the Moon, with Neil Armstrong becoming the first human to walk on it.

2000.title: Y2K Millennium
2000.description: The year 2000 marks the new millennium with widespread digital celebrations worldwide.

2020.title: Modern Era
2020.description: Humanity continues to advance through technology, space exploration, and renewable energy.

-4000.title: Early Bronze Age
-4000.description: Bronze Age civilizations emerge in the Near East. Early forms of writing and organized agriculture spread.

-3900.title: Rise of Mesopotamia
-3900.description: Sumer emerges as one of the world's first civilizations, developing irrigation and early writing systems.

-3800.title: Egyptian Dynasties
-3800.description: Early Egyptian dynasties establish themselves along the Nile River, creating a powerful civilization.

-3700.title: Akkadian Expansion
-3700.description: The Akkadian empire grows in influence, spreading Semitic culture throughout Mesopotamia.

-3600.title: Development of Script
-3600.description: Writing systems develop in multiple civilizations, allowing for record-keeping and literature.

-3500.title: First Cities
-3500.description: The first true cities emerge in Mesopotamia. Complex societies and trade networks develop.

-3400.title: Ancient Commerce
-3400.description: Long-distance trade networks connect Mesopotamia, Egypt, and the Indus Valley.

-3300.title: Pyramids Begin
-3300.description: Construction of monumental pyramids begins in Egypt, showcasing advanced engineering and organization.

-3200.title: Bronze Technology
-3200.description: Bronze working techniques spread widely, creating stronger tools and weapons than copper.

-3100.title: Egyptian Unification
-3100.description: Upper and Lower Egypt unite under a single pharaoh, establishing the Old Kingdom.

-3000.title: Rise of Egyptian Civilization
-3000.description: The Old Kingdom of Egypt reaches its peak. The Great Pyramid of Giza stands as a testament to Egyptian power.

-2900.title: Palace Culture
-2900.description: Elaborate palace complexes are built in Minoan Crete, marking the height of early Aegean civilization.

-2800.title: Great Pyramid Era
-2800.description: The height of pyramid construction in Egypt, with massive monuments built by thousands of workers.

-2700.title: Akkadian Power
-2700.description: The Akkadian Empire reaches its zenith under Sargon of Akkad, the first true emperor.

-2600.title: Harappan Growth
-2600.description: The Indus Valley civilization expands, with cities like Mohenjo-daro becoming major trade centers.

-2500.title: Indus Valley Civilization
-2500.description: The Harappan civilization flourishes with sophisticated urban planning and drainage systems.

-2400.title: Babylonian Rise
-2400.description: Babylon emerges as a powerful city-state in Mesopotamia, beginning its ascent to dominance.

-2300.title: Egyptian Decline
-2300.description: The Old Kingdom of Egypt declines as the power of pharaohs weakens.

-2200.title: Ur-Nammu's Law
-2200.description: The Code of Ur-Nammu, one of the oldest known law codes, is established in ancient Sumer.

-2100.title: Ur's Golden Age
-2100.description: The city of Ur becomes the dominant power in Mesopotamia under the Third Dynasty of Ur.

-2000.title: Shang Dynasty China
-2000.description: The Shang Dynasty rules China, developing early forms of writing and advanced bronze technology.

-1900.title: Amorite Dominance
-1900.description: Amorite peoples gain control of Mesopotamian city-states, establishing new dynasties.

-1800.title: Babylonian Empire
-1800.description: Babylon becomes the dominant power in Mesopotamia under Hammurabi and his legal code.

-1700.title: Hittite Kingdom
-1700.description: The Hittite Empire rises in Anatolia, developing iron working technology and powerful armies.

-1600.title: Egyptian Renaissance
-1600.description: The New Kingdom begins in Egypt, marked by powerful pharaohs and imperial expansion.

-1500.title: Mycenaean Greece
-1500.description: Bronze Age Greece flourishes with the Mycenaean civilization at its height of power.

-1400.title: Minoan Culture
-1400.description: The Minoan civilization of Crete develops advanced art, architecture, and maritime trade.

-1300.title: Trojan War Era
-1300.description: The legendary Trojan War period occurs, as recorded in Homer's epics.

-1200.title: Bronze Age Collapse
-1200.description: Major civilizations decline, including the Mycenaean and Hittite empires, ushering in the Iron Age.

-1100.title: Dark Ages Begin
-1100.description: Greece enters a period of cultural decline and reduced urbanization following the Bronze Age collapse.

-1000.title: Iron Age Begins
-1000.description: The Iron Age spreads across Europe and Asia, revolutionizing tool and weapon making.

-900.title: Assyrian Empire
-900.description: The Assyrian Empire begins its rise to power in Mesopotamia with military innovations.

-800.title: Founding of Rome
-800.description: According to legend, Rome is founded by Romulus on the Tiber River in Italy.

-700.title: Homeric Epic Age
-700.description: Homer composes the Iliad and Odyssey, foundational works of Western literature.

-600.title: Confucius Born
-600.description: Confucius is born in China, eventually becoming one of history's most influential philosophers.

-500.title: Classical Athens
-500.description: Athens becomes the center of Greek civilization during its Golden Age under Pericles.

-400.title: Peloponnesian War
-400.description: The Peloponnesian War rages between Athens and Sparta, reshaping Greek politics.

-300.title: Alexander the Great
-300.description: Alexander the Great conquers the Persian Empire, spreading Greek culture across the known world.

-200.title: Han Dynasty
-200.description: The Han Dynasty rules China, establishing the Silk Road and advancing technology.

-100.title: Roman Expansion
-100.description: Rome expands its empire across the Mediterranean, becoming the dominant world power.

-50.title: Julius Caesar
-50.description: Julius Caesar conquers Gaul and becomes one of Rome's most powerful generals, reshaping the republic.

-6.title: Birth of Jesus Christ
-6.description: Jesus Christ is born in Bethlehem, Judea. His teachings will eventually become the foundation of Christianity.

grassland.description: Grassland: Movement cost 1, Food +2, Production +0, Trade +0

plains.description: Plains: Open terrain good for resources and trade routes. Food +1, Production +1, Trade +0

desert.description: Desert: Harsh terrain with potential for gold deposits. Food +0, Production +1, Trade +0

forest.description: Forest: Dense woodland that provides production but slows movement. Food +1, Production +2, Trade +0

hills.description: Hills: Elevated terrain good for mining and defense. Food +1, Production +1, Trade +0

mountains.description: Mountains: Impassable terrain rich in gold and iron. Excellent for mining operations. Food +0, Production +3, Trade +0

ocean.description: Ocean: Deep water that blocks land units but provides fish and trade opportunities. Food +1, Production +0, Trade +2

river.description: River: Fresh flowing water that provides fish and supports nearby agriculture. Food +2, Production +0, Trade +1

jungle.description: Jungle: Dense tropical vegetation that slows movement but may hide valuable resources. Food +1, Production +1, Trade +0

arctic.description: Permanently frozen ice and snow. Cold and hostile terrain with limited resources but may provide seal hunting. Movement cost: 2, Food: 1, Production: 0, Trade: 0

swamp.description: Wetlands filled with murky water and marsh vegetation. Difficult to traverse and unhealthy, but may provide fish resources. Movement cost: 2, Food: 1, Production: 0, Trade: 0

tundra.description: Cold, partially frozen ground with sparse vegetation. Harsh climate with limited food production but may provide game. Movement cost: 1, Food: 1, Production: 0, Trade: 0
